prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial is shown in black and white.

At the beginning, the image presents a staged, decorative scene. A smiling female figure, possibly a performer or doll-like character, stands in the center wearing a very full, ruffled dress. Behind her is a large fan-shaped backdrop with bold dark-and-light stripes. To the right and behind her are oversized food items: a plate holding several baked goods or pastries, and a glass or cup on a saucer. The composition looks theatrical or fanciful, with the figure placed among enlarged food props.

By the final frame, the commercial has shifted to a product display. Three cylindrical Pillsbury refrigerated-dough cans are arranged against a plain background. The visible labels include “Pillsbury Buttermilk Biscuits” on the upper can, “Pillsbury Cinnamon Swirl” on the left can, and “Pillsbury Quick Orange Rolls” on the lower right can. A rectangular sign beside the products reads: “at your grocer’s dairy case.” The final image functions as a clear product pack shot, showing the items and where they can be found.
